content.full_text:
CITY OF MOUNTAIN VIEW

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY                                P. O. Box 7540
Telephone: 415.903.6303                        Mountain View, CA 94039
FAX: 415.967.4215

December 11, 1990

VIA FAX

Susan Bobrow, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for Advice letter--Potential Conflict of Interest

Dear Susan:

This follows up a telephone inquiry on December 11, 1990, as I was
unable to reach you. I am seeking advice based on the following
facts.

Councilmember Dena Bonnell performs financial consulting services
for an independent oil products distribution firm (Oil Co). From that
work she derives (considerably) more than $250 per year in
compensation. Ms. Bonnell was previously an employee of Oil Co.

Oil Co. has an ongoing relationship with a large, statewide financial
institution (Bank), which includes maintaining business bank
accounts, and providing almost all financing for the day-to-day
operations of Oil Co. Bank is one of the three or four largest banks
doing business in the state and has a major market share of
California banking business. Bank's financing is a major necessity in
Oil Co.'s business operations, i.e. Bank supplies well over $1000
annually in financing to Oil Co.

Because of the ongoing financing arrangement, Bank has considerable
influence on Oil Co.'s business decisions. Oil Co.'s accounts are
serviced from Bank's downtown Mountain View branch.
Councilmember Bonnell also maintains several personal "consumer"

Susan Bobrow, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
December 11, 1990
Page 2

bank accounts at Bank's downtown branch, but does not borrow
money from Bank(except perhaps through a consumer credit card
account).

The City is considering a project to construct a new Library in
downtown Mountain View. One of the sites under consideration
(among several) is the site of Bank's branch. If that site were
selected, acquisition by the City would be necessary, either by
negotiated purchase or eminent domain. Bank's position regarding
acquisition is unknown at this time.

The question is whether the above described relationship between
Oil Co., Bank and Councilmember Bonnell would create a conflict of
interest for the Councilmember if she were to consider and act on the
selection of Bank's site for the proposed library. Because Bank's
major relationship is with Oil Co., I do not see a conflict under any of
the "direct" conflict provisions of the Act. Guideline sec. 18702,6--
"indirect involvement"--appears as though it might have relevance.
Whether it is reasonably foreseeable that acquisition of the site of a
branch of a major statewide bank would have an effect on Oil Co.'s
source of financing income is something I have not been able to
determine with clarity.

It would be appreciated if an advice letter responding to this inquiry
could be prepared as soon as possible. Presentation of the site
selection issue to the City Council is expected to occur in early
January. 1991.

If you have any questions, please let me know.

Very truly yours,

P. Lawrence Klose
City Attorney

cc: Councilmember Bonnell

sections.question: Whether the described relationship between Oil Co., Bank and Councilmember Bonnell would create a conflict of interest for the Councilmember if she were to consider and act on the selection of Bank's site for the proposed library. Specifically, whether acquisition of the site of a branch of a major statewide bank would have an effect on Oil Co.'s source of financing income such that an indirect conflict exists under Guideline sec. 18702.6.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Councilmember Bonnell have a conflict of interest in voting on a potential City library project site located at a Bank branch, given her financial consulting relationship with Oil Co. (which receives substantial financing from Bank) and her personal banking relationship with Bank?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city attorney to FPPC seeking an advice letter; no FPPC response or conclusion sections are present. The request includes detailed factual scenario and cites to specific guideline (18702.6) regarding indirect involvement. Time-sensitive matter with early January 1991 deadline mentioned.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether the described relationship between Oil Co., Bank and Councilmember Bonnell would create a conflict of interest for the Councilmember if she were to consider and act on the selection of Bank's site for the proposed library. Specifically, whether acquisition of the site of a branch of a major statewide bank would have an effect on Oil Co.'s source of financing income such that an indirect conflict exists under Guideline sec. 18702.6.

Lawrence Klose City Attorney cc: Councilmember Bonnell
embedding.summary: City Attorney for Mountain View requests FPPC advice regarding whether Councilmember Dena Bonnell has a conflict of interest in considering a library site selection that may involve a bank branch, given her financial consulting income from an oil company that depends on financing from that same bank.
